--------------
Focus on deliverables and capabilities:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- If file related research comes back as empty or not found
- **No output necessary if skipping.**

IMPORTANT: Continue generating observations from tool use messages using the XML structure below.

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

```xml
<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
      - feature: new capability or functionality added
      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->

  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>

    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>

    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->

  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>

    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made

      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision
```

</summary>

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <what_happened>Write</what_happened>
  <occurred_at>2026-03-02T10:27:13.823Z</occurred_at>
  <working_directory>/Users/royrigamonti/columbia-site</working_directory>
  <parameters>"{\"file_path\":\"/Users/royrigamonti/columbia-site/CLAUDE.md\",\"content\":\"# CLAUDE.md\\n\\nThis file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.\\n\\n## Development Commands\\n\\n### Getting Started\\n- **Dev server**: `npm run dev` - Starts development server at http://localhost:3000 with hot reload\\n- **Build**: `npm run build` - Creates optimized production build\\n- **Start production**: `npm start` - Runs production server (requires build first)\\n- **Lint**: `npm run lint` - Runs ESLint on the codebase\\n\\n## Architecture\\n\\n### Technology Stack\\n- **Next.js 16.1.6**: React framework with App Router (not Pages Router)\\n- **React 19.2.3**: UI library\\n- **TypeScript 5**: Type-safe development\\n- **Tailwind CSS 4**: Utility-first CSS framework\\n- **ESLint 9**: Code quality with Next.js + TypeScript configs\\n\\n### Project Structure\\n```\\napp/                 # Next.js App Router directory\\n├── layout.tsx      # Root layout (shared across all pages)\\n├── page.tsx        # Home page (routes to /)\\n├── globals.css     # Global Tailwind CSS imports\\n└── favicon.ico     # Favicon\\npublic/            # Static assets served at root\\n```\\n\\n### Key Architectural Patterns\\n- **App Router**: This project uses Next.js 13+ App Router structure (not Pages Router). All routes are defined in the `app` directory with `page.tsx` files.\\n- **Server Components by Default**: React Server Components are the default in Next.js App Router. Use `'use client'` directive only when you need interactivity (hooks, event listeners, etc.).\\n- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS configured with @tailwindcss/postcss v4. Utility classes are the primary styling approach.\\n- **Type Safety**: Strict TypeScript (`\\\"strict\\\": true` in tsconfig.json) enforces type checking throughout the application.\\n\\n## Development Notes\\n\\n### Path Aliases\\nThe project is configured with `@/*` path alias mapping to the root directory. Use `@/app/...` to import components instead of relative paths.\\n\\n### Adding New Pages\\nTo add a new page:\\n1. Create a new directory in `app/` (e.g., `app/about/`)\\n2. Create `page.tsx` in that directory\\n3. The route will automatically be `/about`\\n\\n### Adding Components\\nCreate reusable components and organize them logically. Consider whether they need `'use client'` based on interactivity requirements.\\n\\n### ESLint Configuration\\nThe project uses ESLint with Next.js recommended rules and TypeScript support. Configuration includes:\\n- Core Web Vitals rules\\n- TypeScript-specific rules\\n- Next.js best practices\\n\\nRun `npm run lint` before committing to catch issues early.\\n\\n## No Test Framework Currently\\nThe project doesn't currently have a test framework set up (no Jest, Vitest, etc.). If tests are needed, consider adding a testing framework compatible with Next.js.\\n\"}"</parameters>
  <outcome>"{\"type\":\"create\",\"filePath\":\"/Users/royrigamonti/columbia-site/CLAUDE.md\",\"content\":\"# CLAUDE.md\\n\\nThis file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.\\n\\n## Development Commands\\n\\n### Getting Started\\n- **Dev server**: `npm run dev` - Starts development server at http://localhost:3000 with hot reload\\n- **Build**: `npm run build` - Creates optimized production build\\n- **Start production**: `npm start` - Runs production server (requires build first)\\n- **Lint**: `npm run lint` - Runs ESLint on the codebase\\n\\n## Architecture\\n\\n### Technology Stack\\n- **Next.js 16.1.6**: React framework with App Router (not Pages Router)\\n- **React 19.2.3**: UI library\\n- **TypeScript 5**: Type-safe development\\n- **Tailwind CSS 4**: Utility-first CSS framework\\n- **ESLint 9**: Code quality with Next.js + TypeScript configs\\n\\n### Project Structure\\n```\\napp/                 # Next.js App Router directory\\n├── layout.tsx      # Root layout (shared across all pages)\\n├── page.tsx        # Home page (routes to /)\\n├── globals.css     # Global Tailwind CSS imports\\n└── favicon.ico     # Favicon\\npublic/            # Static assets served at root\\n```\\n\\n### Key Architectural Patterns\\n- **App Router**: This project uses Next.js 13+ App Router structure (not Pages Router). All routes are defined in the `app` directory with `page.tsx` files.\\n- **Server Components by Default**: React Server Components are the default in Next.js App Router. Use `'use client'` directive only when you need interactivity (hooks, event listeners, etc.).\\n- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS configured with @tailwindcss/postcss v4. Utility classes are the primary styling approach.\\n- **Type Safety**: Strict TypeScript (`\\\"strict\\\": true` in tsconfig.json) enforces type checking throughout the application.\\n\\n## Development Notes\\n\\n### Path Aliases\\nThe project is configured with `@/*` path alias mapping to the root directory. Use `@/app/...` to import components instead of relative paths.\\n\\n### Adding New Pages\\nTo add a new page:\\n1. Create a new directory in `app/` (e.g., `app/about/`)\\n2. Create `page.tsx` in that directory\\n3. The route will automatically be `/about`\\n\\n### Adding Components\\nCreate reusable components and organize them logically. Consider whether they need `'use client'` based on interactivity requirements.\\n\\n### ESLint Configuration\\nThe project uses ESLint with Next.js recommended rules and TypeScript support. Configuration includes:\\n- Core Web Vitals rules\\n- TypeScript-specific rules\\n- Next.js best practices\\n\\nRun `npm run lint` before committing to catch issues early.\\n\\n## No Test Framework Currently\\nThe project doesn't currently have a test framework set up (no Jest, Vitest, etc.). If tests are needed, consider adding a testing framework compatible with Next.js.\\n\",\"structuredPatch\":[],\"originalFile\":null}"</outcome>

Write progress notes of what was done, what was learned, and what's next. This is a checkpoint to capture progress so far. ### 📋 Contents:

**Development Commands**
- Dev server, build, production start, and lint commands

**Architecture**
- Technology stack overview (Next.js 16.1.6, React 19, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS)

**Development Notes**
- Path aliases (`@/*` configuration)
- How to add new pages (using Next.js App Router conventions)
- Component organization guidance
- ESLint configuration details
- Note about lack of test framework

The file is concise and focuses on what a future instance of Claude Code needs to know to be productive in this repository—without being obvious or repetitive. It highlights the important architectural decision (App Router vs Pages Router) and explains when Server Components vs Client Components are needed.

Respond in this XML format:
<summary>

  <learned>[What have you learned about how things work?]</learned>

  <next_steps>[What are you actively working on or planning to work on next in this session?]</next_steps>
  <notes>[Additional insights or observations about the current progress]</notes>
</summary>

IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this next PROGRESS SUMMARY - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the summary content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful summary content.

Thank you, this summary will be very useful for keeping track of our progress!
